description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a straight, high-mounted upper wing and a slightly lower parallel lower wing, both supported by struts and wires. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the rear, ending in a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The aircraft features two visible piston engines mounted between the wings, each with a propeller. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tailwheel, all fixed and non-retractable. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and appears to be open, typical of early 20th-century aircraft. The overall surface is smooth and metallic, with a uniform gray color.
description: A vintage biplane with dual piston engines, high-mounted straight wings, and a forward open cockpit. The aircraft is metallic gray with fixed landing gear and a conventional tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the slender fuselage, high-mounted wings, and the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual engines with propellers, the biplane wing structure, and the open cockpit area.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the wing struts and tail.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the alignment of the wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with straight, parallel wings supported by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted between the upper and lower wings, each with a front-facing propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located at the front of the fuselage, likely single or tandem seat.
